name: Chez Michelle Restaurant
about: Authentic African cuisine with a focus on bringing people together. Perfect for late-night Ndole soup.
our_story: We are passionate about sharing the rich flavors and warm hospitality that define African dining using traditional recipes and fresh ingredients.
cultural_roots: Food in African culture is a way of expressing love and preserving generations of traditions.

name: Addis Ababa Ethiopian Restaurant
about: A cozy, inviting hidden gem known for late-night hours and vibrant communal dining.
our_story: Founded to preserve traditional Habesha recipes where the aroma of berbere and fresh injera welcomes everyone.
cultural_roots: Honors the 'Agelgil' tradition where sharing a plate signifies bonds of friendship.

name: Famous Princess African Kitchen
address: 17036 W Little York Rd, Houston, TX 77084
about: High-end Nigerian cuisine specializing in bold spices and meticulous preparation of traditional stews.
our_story: Showcasing the 'touch of difference' by using the finest ingredients to create stews that taste like home.
cultural_roots: Celebrates 'Swallow' culture—dishes like Amala and Pounded Yam paired with nutrient-rich soups.

name: Omalicha Kitchen
address: 1701 Cypress Creek Pkwy Ste D, Houston, TX 77090
about: A vibrant hub for Igbo delicacies and seafood in a modern, welcoming environment.
our_story: Dedicated to bringing regional flavors of Eastern Nigeria, like Oha and Afang soups, to Houston.
cultural_roots: Celebrates the industrious spirit of the Igbo people through fresh, earthy ingredients.

name: Kokomo
about: A vibrant, multisensory Caribbean experience in Brooklyn known for high energy and bold, refined island flavors.
our_story: Kokomo was designed to celebrate the joy of the Caribbean through food, music, and art, creating a 'vacation in a meal' for every guest.
cultural_roots: Inspired by the diverse islands of the West Indies, blending traditional spices with modern Brooklyn flair.

name: DJONDJON
about: Warm, relaxed Haitian restaurant/bar (also known as Zanmi on their site). Daily brunch, weekend live music, and Haitian specialties. 'Zanmi' means 'Friend' in Kreyòl.
our_story: Our mission is to showcase the beauty of Haiti through upscale comfort food, moving beyond stereotypes to highlight the depth of our culinary history.
cultural_roots: Haitian cuisine is a proud blend of African, French, and Taino traditions, celebrated here through slow-stewed meats and aromatic 'epis'.

name: Dukunoo Jamaican Kitchen
about: An upscale, artsy Jamaican destination in Wynwood that invites you to 'come for the food, stay for the vibes.'
our_story: Named after a traditional sweet treat, Dukunoo offers the 'sweet surprise' of authentic Jamaican wood-fired jerk in a chic, modern setting.
cultural_roots: Honors the 'Out of Many, One People' heritage of Jamaica, blending Taino smoke techniques with global presentation.

name: Apt 4B
about: A retro-cool, vinyl-filled Caribbean hideaway that feels like an intimate Brooklyn apartment party in the 70s.
our_story: We created a space where music and food are inseparable, featuring one of the largest vinyl collections in the South alongside elite fusion dishes.
cultural_roots: A 'love letter' to the West Indian diaspora, blending French technique with the soulful spices of the islands.

name: Blaqhaus
address: 1167 Joseph E. Boone Blvd NW, Atlanta, GA 30314
about: A fusion of Southern comfort and Caribbean heat, offering a high-energy dining experience in the heart of Atlanta.
our_story: Blaqhaus was born from the desire to bridge the gap between Soul Food and Island spices, creating a community hub for food and nightlife.
cultural_roots: Celebrates the historical connection between the American South and the Caribbean islands through shared ingredients like corn, greens, and peppers.

name: Callaloo Trinidadian Kitchen
about: A no-frills, legendary neighborhood spot serving some of the most authentic Trinidadian street food in NYC.
our_story: We keep it simple: authentic recipes from Port of Spain, served with speed and heart to our Brooklyn community.
cultural_roots: Trinidadian cuisine is a unique mix of African and Indian heritage, famous for hand-held delights and complex curries.

name: Miss Lily's 7A
about: A high-energy Jamaican diner and juice bar with a colorful, island-shack aesthetic in the East Village.
our_story: Miss Lily's is a cultural destination where old-school ska meets modern Caribbean cooking, providing a vibrant escape from the city streets.
cultural_roots: Honors the roadside 'jerk centers' of Jamaica while adding a cosmopolitan New York energy.

name: Negrit
about: A modern Caribbean lounge in Houston's Midtown specializing in creative cocktails and island-inspired small plates.
our_story: Negrit was built to offer a sophisticated alternative to island dining, focusing on the craft of the cocktail and the artistry of the platter.
cultural_roots: A celebration of the 'Black' (Negrit) influence on global cuisine, focusing specifically on the Afro-Caribbean experience.

name: Cane
specialty: Tiffin Box with Curry Goat & Paratha
about: Chef Peter Prime brings the soul of Trinidadian night markets to a sleek DC storefront. The 'Tiffin Box' is a legendary multi-layered experience of curries and chutneys.
our_story: Chef Peter Prime named the restaurant after the sugar cane that shaped the history and cuisine of his native Trinidad.
cultural_roots: Explores the diverse heritage of Trinidad, highlighting the influence of Indian indentured servants and enslaved Africans on the island's flavors.
menu_highlights: Tiffin Box with Curry Goat & Paratha

name: Pearl's
about: A chic Williamsburg spot serving 'Trini-style' seafood and tropical cocktails in a bright, beachy atmosphere.
our_story: Pearl's is our tribute to the coastal flavors of the islands, designed to bring the warmth of a Caribbean beach house to the middle of Brooklyn.
cultural_roots: Focuses on the 'Blue Food' (seafood) traditions of the Caribbean, where fresh catch and island spices rule the table.

name: Appioo African Bar & Grill
about: A beloved DC staple offering authentic Ghanaian home-style cooking and a lively communal bar scene.
our_story: Chef Prince Boateng created Appioo to bring the 'chop bar' culture of Accra to the U Street corridor, focusing on recipes passed down through generations.
cultural_roots: Celebrates the soul of Ghana, where meals like Fufu and Light Soup are not just food, but a centerpiece for community and storytelling.

name: Teranga
about: A modern African superfood café located in The Africa Center, overlooking Central Park.
our_story: Founded by world-renowned Chef Pierre Thiam, Teranga reflects the Senegalese philosophy of hospitality, offering nutrient-dense bowls based on ancient grains.
cultural_roots: The word 'Teranga' means 'hospitality' in Wolof. The menu highlights fonio and jollof rice as the foundation of West African agricultural heritage.

name: Mansa Kunda
about: An upscale dining destination celebrating the rich history and flavors of The Gambia and Senegal.
our_story: Named after 'Mansa' (King) and 'Kunda' (Home/Family), we strive to treat every guest like royalty while serving authentic dishes from the Jolof Empire roots.
cultural_roots: Focuses on the deep culinary connection between the land and the river in Senegambian culture, utilizing smoked fish and peanut-based sauces.

name: Bukom Cafe
about: A legendary Adams Morgan haunt known for its spicy West African dishes and live Reggae and Afrobeat music.
our_story: For over 20 years, Bukom has been the heartbeat of DC's African nightlife, blending authentic spices with a high-energy lounge atmosphere.
cultural_roots: A melting pot of West African flavors, honoring the late-night dining culture found in the urban hubs of Lagos and Accra.

name: Ponti's Ivorian Kitchen
about: The go-to spot in Houston for authentic Ivorian (Ivory Coast) street food and grilled delicacies.
our_story: Ponti's was built to satisfy the cravings of the Ivorian diaspora, specializing in the slow-grilled techniques that define the streets of Abidjan.
cultural_roots: Highlights Ivorian staples like Attiéké (fermented cassava) and Aloco, showing the unique French-African culinary marriage.

name: Gold Coast African Restaurant
about: A Chicago staple for over 20 years, providing a nostalgic taste of Ghana's finest traditional meals.
our_story: We are one of the oldest African restaurants in the city, dedicated to maintaining the exact flavor profiles of the 'Gold Coast' for our long-time patrons.
cultural_roots: Roots deep in the Ashanti and Fante cooking traditions, where hearty soups and fufu are the ultimate comfort foods.

name: Afrik-CHe Kitchen
about: A casual, highly-rated spot known for its consistent quality and generous portions of West African staples.
our_story: We believe that quality African food should be accessible. Our kitchen focuses on the 'Big Three': Jollof, Soup, and Swallow.
cultural_roots: Representing the pan-African spirit of Houston's Bissonnet district, where food acts as the universal language.

name: Adulis
about: An authentic Eritrean dining room in Queens known for its warm hospitality and traditional clay-pot cooking.
our_story: Named after the ancient port city of Adulis, we aim to bridge the gap between ancient Red Sea history and modern New York through the art of the spice.
cultural_roots: Eritrean cuisine shares similarities with Ethiopian food but features unique coastal influences and a distinct blend of 'Tsebhi' (stews) served on injera.

name: Kulan Cafe
address: 3821 S George Mason Dr, Falls Church, VA 22041
about: East African restaurant in Build America Plaza. Kulan Cafe is famous for its massive portions and signature spiced goat, plus Somali tea and rice dishes.
our_story: We built Kulan to be a gathering place—a 'Kulan'—where people can start their day with Somali tea and end it with a mountain of rice and tender meat.
cultural_roots: Somali cuisine is a crossroads of flavors from the Horn of Africa, India, and Italy, characterized by the use of 'Xawaash' spice mix and the inevitable banana served on the side.

name: Safari Restaurant
about: An award-winning Somali upscale restaurant that has become a national landmark for East African cuisine in the Midwest.
our_story: Safari was founded to elevate Somali food from casual cafes to a formal dining experience, proving that our traditional recipes belong in the fine-dining world.
cultural_roots: Showcases the nomadic heritage of the Somali people, where grilled meats and fragrant rice are staples of hospitality and survival.

name: 10 Degrees South
about: The first South African fine-dining restaurant in the US, offering a sophisticated taste of Johannesburg in Buckhead.
our_story: The Anthony family moved from South Africa to Atlanta with a mission to introduce Americans to the 'finer side' of the continent's southern tip.
cultural_roots: A sophisticated blend of French, Portuguese, and Indian influences that have simmered in South Africa for centuries.

name: Tatiana by Kwame Onwuachi
address: 10 Lincoln Center Plaza, New York, NY 10023
quality_factor: Named the #1 Restaurant in NYC (Kwame Onwuachi)
about: Elite fine dining at Lincoln Center, where Chef Kwame Onwuachi creates the intersection of heritage and high-fashion dining with specialties like Wagyu Short Rib Pastrami.

name: ChòpnBlọk
quality_factor: The "Gold Standard" for West African fast-fine dining.
about: Accessible West African flavors for the modern palate.

name: Virgil's Gullah Kitchen
about: A love letter to the coastal heritage of the Gullah people. West Midtown.

name: Ethiopic
about: Authentic Ethiopian cuisine in the heart of Washington, DC. First Ethiopian on the H Street Corridor.

name: Desta
about: Where traditional happiness meets contemporary design. Authentic Ethiopian since 2006; also Westside Village location.

name: Busy Bee Cafe
address: 810 Martin Luther King Jr Dr SW, Atlanta, GA 30314
about: A legendary Atlanta institution since 1947. Michelin Bib Gourmand & James Beard Award winner; soul food and Southern comfort.

name: Meals by Genet
address: 1053 S Fairfax Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90019
about: Women-owned; legendary Dorowat in the West. Little Ethiopia; Fri–Sun 6–9pm, reservations recommended.

name: African Chop
about: Cameroonian food truck; jollof, suya, puff puff. Wed–Sat 11:30am–9:30pm; catering (323) 607-9586.

name: BlaqHaus NoHo
about: Southern comfort and Caribbean heat; weekend brunch, Rum Room Thu–Sat. Women-owned.

name: Amawele's
about: Durban-style South African; peri peri, bunny chow. Rincon Center. Now also subscription/catering.

name: Demera Ethiopian
quality_factor: A Chicago institution for elite Ethiopian dining.
about: Chef Tigist Reda is a culinary ambassador for Ethiopia in the Midwest. Demera offers a polished, full-service experience that is widely considered the best Ethiopian in Chicago.

name: African Small Pot
about: West African (Mauritanian, Senegalese, Nigerian). Wood-grilled Diby; open daily 8am–1am.

name: Gorée Cuisine
about: Authentic Senegalese; Yassa chicken, maffe, grilled fish. Kenwood/Hyde Park border.

name: Kilimandjaro
about: Senegalese by Youma Ba; thieboudienne, yassa, mafée. Reopened 2024 at LVL West. BYOB.

name: Iyanze
about: The heart of the diaspora in the heart of the city. Largest Nigerian restaurant in Chicago; 40+ dishes weekly.

name: Yassa African Restaurant
about: Authentic Senegalese since 2004; yassa chicken, jollof, grilled fish. Bronzeville; catering & live music.

name: Quality Taste
about: Family-owned Jamaican; jerk, curry goat, oxtail. Belmont, West Philly.

name: Communion R&B
about: An award-winning soul-fusion restaurant in Seattle's Central District, serving innovative dishes like Hood Sushi & Catfish in a vibrant, social atmosphere.

name: Meskel Ethiopian
about: 30+ years in Central District; injera, kitfo, doro wat. Little Addis Ababa.

name: Cesaria
about: Cape Verdean since 2002; katchupa, live morna. Named for Cesaria Evora.

name: Cafe Selam
address: 2715 E Cherry St B, Seattle, WA 98122
about: 20+ years; house-made injera, organic. Central District. Catering.

name: Suya Joint
about: Beef suya, jollof, egusi; Best of Boston. Chef Cecelia Lizotte.

name: Jebena Cafe
about: Woman-owned; Ethiopian cuisine & Jebena Coffee Co. Northgate.

name: Cool Runnings Jamaican Grill
about: A legendary Houston staple. It has national fame from 'Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives' but stays true to its roots.

name: Bennachin
about: Authentic West African (Gambian & Cameroonian) in the French Quarter since 1992.

name: Dooky Chase's
about: Historic Creole institution since 1941 in Treme; civil rights gathering place.

name: Abyssinia
about: Authentic Ethiopian cuisine in the heart of Manhattan, serving traditional dishes with fresh injera and bold spices.

name: Teranga (The Village)
about: Senegalese by Chef Nafy Flatley; Maafe bowl, jollof, tamarind chicken. Mon–Fri 10:30am–3pm.

name: Lala's Eritrean
about: Eritrean in Temescal (also listed as Cafe Eritrea D'Afrique).

name: Riddim n' Spice
about: Jamaican brothers; jerk chicken, Rum Room Thu–Sat. North Nashville.

name: Reggae Hut
about: Caribbean & soul; brown stew fish, jerk chicken, patties. Black-owned.

name: Caribbean Hotpot Grill

secondary_location.name: Veterans Memorial
secondary_location.address: 12005 Veterans Memorial Dr, Ste A, Houston, TX 77067

about: Authentic Caribbean cuisine with vibrant flavors and island vibes. Serving traditional Jamaican and Caribbean dishes in a lively, welcoming atmosphere.

name: Reggae Hut (Veterans Memorial)
address: 12005 Veterans Memorial Dr, Ste A, Houston, TX 77067
about: Authentic Jamaican cuisine in Houston, serving traditional Caribbean dishes with bold flavors and island vibes.

name: Spice House Midtown
about: A lively Caribbean fusion restaurant in Midtown Atlanta, known for innovative dishes like Jerk Chicken Pasta in a vibrant, social atmosphere.

name: Jasmine's Caribbean Cuisine
about: A tropical escape in Hell's Kitchen, serving authentic Caribbean cuisine with specialties like Curry Shrimp and Red Snapper.

name: Garifuna Flava
about: An authentic, family-owned gem in Marquette Park, serving traditional Belizean cuisine including specialties like Belizean Stew Chicken.

name: St. James - Modern Caribbean
about: A sophisticated and chic modern Caribbean restaurant in the U Street / 14th St area, known for elevated dishes like Callaloo & Crab.

name: Dulan's on Crenshaw
about: A community landmark in Hyde Park, serving authentic soul food with specialties like Smothered Chicken in a welcoming, family-friendly atmosphere.

name: Chef Creole
about: Authentic Haitian cuisine in Little Haiti, serving traditional dishes like Griot & Pikliz in a tropical, authentic atmosphere.

name: Red Rooster Overtown
specialty: Coconut Smoked Oxtails & M's Fried Yardbird
quality_factor: Marcus Samuelsson's tribute to Black culinary history.
about: Marcus Samuelsson's tribute to Black culinary history. Features a world-class art collection and the famous 'Shrine' Saturday nights for Afrobeats.

name: Baobab Fare
about: An award-winning Burundian restaurant in New Center, serving heartfelt East African cuisine with specialties like Kuku Na Mali in a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

name: Lucille's
about: A historic and elegant fine dining restaurant in Houston's Museum District, serving elevated Southern cuisine with specialties like Chili Biscuits & Yardbird in a sophisticated setting.

name: Ja' Grill Hyde Park
about: An upscale and energetic Jamaican restaurant in Hyde Park, serving authentic Caribbean cuisine with specialties like Curry Goat & Ja' Rum Punch in a vibrant, sophisticated setting.

name: Irie Mon Cafe
about: Jamaican; jerk chicken, ackee & saltfish. Catering, food truck at 530 Joseph E Lowery Blvd.

name: Dōgon
about: High-end Afro-Caribbean fine dining by the Potomac, recognized by Michelin 2025.

name: Dakar NOLA
about: A single-seating, multi-course dining experience that follows the lineage of West African food to New Orleans. Reservations are highly sought after.

name: The Jerk Shack
about: Chef Nicola Blaque; jerk chicken, curry, patties. Michelin Bib Gourmand.

name: Kann
specialty: Glazed Duck with Cane Sugar & Habanero
menu_highlights: Glazed Duck with Cane Sugar & Habanero
awards: James Beard Best New Restaurant Winner
quality_factor: America's most famous Haitian wood-fired kitchen.
about: Chef Gregory Gourdet's masterpiece. This is arguably the most famous Haitian restaurant in the world right now, featuring an open kitchen fueled entirely by wood fire.

name: Canje
address: 1914 E 6th St Ste C, Austin, TX 78702
about: Named after the national bird of Guyana, Canje delivers a Michelin-worthy exploration of the Caribbean. The space is vibrant and lush, perfect for an upscale night out on 6th Street.

name: Ember Kitchen
address: 800 W Cesar Chavez St Ste PP110, Austin, TX 78701
about: A live-fire culinary theater using a signature Josper oven. The vibe is sleek, dark, and intimate, overlooking Lady Bird Lake—ideal for a formal celebratory dinner.

name: Aldeez Afribbean
about: A 3,000 sq. ft. creekside oasis that blends authentic West African staples with Caribbean classics. The VIP lounge and live 'Afribbean' Friday nights make this a true destination.

name: The Island Spot (Oak Cliff)
specialty: Mama Joyce's Oxtails & Jerk BBQ Ribs
about: A neighborhood pillar that brings a professional, full-service touch to Jamaican hospitality. Known for their 'Jerk & Jazz' events and deep selection of island rums.

name: Island Spice Bar & Grill
address: 17802 W Little York Rd, Houston, TX 77084
about: A premier Houston destination for those who want a polished dining room that turns into a high-energy lounge at night. The seasoning here is notoriously bold.

name: Habesha Ethiopian
about: Family-owned; fluffy injera, doro wat, tej. Live music.

name: Cafe Nubia
about: African-Mediterranean fusion; Rasta Pasta, suya, brunch, live music. Opened 2024.

name: Shuri African Restaurant
about: Counter-service West African kitchen known for generous portions and staples like egusi, fufu, and pepper soup.

name: Whittier Cafe
about: Denver's African espresso bar—Ethiopian and East African coffees, light fare, Sunday traditional coffee ceremony.

name: Bintü Atelier
specialty: Heritage Jollof & Goat Egusi with Fufu
about: Chef Bintou N'Daw brings a level of precision and elegance to West African staples that matches the legendary fine-dining scene of Charleston.

name: Status Hollywood
address: 1612 N Cahuenga Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90028
specialty: Sweet Chili Lamb Chops & Oxtail Egg Rolls
about: Where Hollywood glamour meets authentic island heat. This is the spot for 'Vibe Dining'—the food quality is high-end, and the crowd is impeccably dressed.

name: Lucian Books and Wine
address: 3003 Peachtree Rd NE Ste 300, Atlanta, GA 30305
specialty: Heritage Pork with Black Garlic & Peanuts
menu_highlights: Heritage Pork with Black Garlic & Peanuts
about: A sophisticated bookstore-restaurant hybrid in Buckhead. It represents the absolute peak of modern, ingredient-driven dining in Atlanta with a world-class wine program.

name: Friday Saturday Sunday
about: Located in a historic Rittenhouse rowhouse, this restaurant offers an eight-course tasting menu that frequently integrates West African and Caribbean seasoning techniques.

name: Jerk Shack
about: Chef Trey Lamont uses high-end techniques to elevate classic street food. The jerk chicken here is seasoned for 48 hours and smoked over authentic pimento wood.

name: Helen's Kitchen
address: 14608 S Post Oak Rd, Houston, TX 77045
about: An authentic Jamaican restaurant in Houston's South Post Oak neighborhood, known for traditional dishes like oxtail and curry goat.

name: Lucia
address: 351 N Fairfax Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90036
about: An elite Afro-Caribbean fine dining destination in LA's Fairfax District, known for innovative dishes like Jerk Lobster and craft cocktails in a late-night lounge atmosphere.

name: Bridgetown Roti
address: 672 S Santa Fe Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90021
about: An award-winning Caribbean American restaurant in LA's Arts District, specializing in authentic roti and patties with a trendy, modern atmosphere.

name: Teranga
about: A polished, health-conscious Senegalese restaurant in San Francisco's Financial District, offering authentic West African dishes like Baobab Energy Bowls and Yassa Chicken.

name: Negril ATL
about: An innovative Caribbean restaurant in Atlanta's Upper West Side, known for creative takes on classic dishes like Ackee & Saltfish Spring Rolls in a high-energy lounge atmosphere.

name: Taste of Nigeria
about: A major player in the Houston scene, known for traditional flavors.

name: Komchop
about: Very consistent quality; a go-to for Westheimer locals.

name: Finger Licking 2.0
about: Famous for their generous portions and 'at-home' taste.

name: Sarabell Calabar
about: Specializes in the rich, seafood-heavy dishes of the Calabar region.

name: Amala Joint
about: Essential for the Katy community; arguably the best Amala in the area.

name: Amala Zone
about: High-quality presentation and a very clean, inviting space.

name: Chef Benny
about: Widely praised for the bold spice levels and consistent seasoning.

name: Afrikiko
address: 9420 Richmond Ave. Ste. E, Houston, TX 77063
about: A legendary family-owned spot for over 15 years. It's the gold standard for Ghanaian comfort food in SW Houston.

name: Dakar Street Food
about: Primary takeout operation in Westchase/Third Ward area. Fast, affordable, and incredibly consistent.

name: Astor's Table
about: Chef Casey brings a unique South African flair to Katy. Great for a casual but 'stepped-up' lunch.

name: Suya Hut
address: 11720 W Airport Blvd, Meadows Place, TX 77477
about: If you just want suya and jollof for a Tuesday night, this is the most reliable spot in the area.

name: Blue Nile Ethiopian
about: The 'Daily Driver' of Ethiopian food. Clean, professional, and has been a staple for years.

name: Aria Suya Kitchen
about: Slightly more 'upscale' than a standard joint, but still very much a regular spot for the Galleria crowd.

name: Jollof Rice King
about: West African spot in Upper Kirby; jollof rice, suya, egusi, pepper soup. Same ownership family as Taste of Nigeria.

name: Butter D' Roti
about: One of the few spots in Katy for authentic Trinidadian Roti. It's a true hidden gem for those in the know.

name: Fabaceae African Cuisine
address: 12611 Woodforest Blvd Ste A, Houston, TX 77015
about: Consistently ranked as one of the best for Yoruba-style Nigerian food. Their designer stew is a fan favorite.

name: Lucy Ethiopian Restaurant & Lounge
about: The perfect 'bridge' between a daily driver and a night out. It has great energy and some of the best tibs in town.

name: Nyammings Fusion Bistro
specialty: Curry Goat & Oxtail Mac n Cheese
about: Perfect for the Richmond/Katy border. They specialize in 'fusion' comfort food that keeps people coming back.

name: Caribbean Hotpot Grill
about: Famous for their massive portions and affordability. It's a true worker's lunch spot.

name: Africa Kine
address: 2267 Adam Clayton Powell Jr Blvd, New York, NY 10027
about: Legendary Senegalese in Harlem since 1994; lamb mafe, chicken yassa, grilled fish. Halal.

name: Buka
about: Best Nigerian in NY; akara, suya, jollof, amala. Bed-Stuy.

name: Chercher
about: Considered the best Ethiopian in the DC area; also DC location. Authentic Injera platters.

name: Veronica's Kitchen
about: Nigerian fufu, egusi, jollof; viral on TikTok. Inglewood legend.

name: Rosalind's
address: 1044 S Fairfax Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90019
about: Historic staple on Ethiopian Row since 1985; vegetarian-friendly and traditional meat dishes.

name: Cafe Songhai
address: 3380 Holcomb Bridge Rd, Peachtree Corners, GA 30092
about: Jollof, waakye, pounded yam & egusi, suya. Wed–Sun. Live entertainment, patio.

name: Belle & Lily's
about: Caribbean brunch house; jerk chicken, mango French toast, bottomless mimosas. Embry Hills.

name: Royal Arm Restaurant
address: 23010 Highland Knolls Dr Ste C, Katy, TX 77494
specialty: Egusi (Melon Stew) with Pounded Yam & Ayamase (Designer Rice)
menu_highlights: Ayamase (Designer Rice) with Boiled Egg & Plantain
about: A local Katy pillar that successfully bridges Nigerian staples with Caribbean favorites. Known for their meticulous preparation of 'swallow' dishes and their standout Designer Rice (Ayamase).

name: Aduke Nigerian Cuisine
about: Best Suya & Fufu in Inglewood. Homemade from scratch; vegan/vegetarian options.

name: T's Buka
about: Jollof, egusi, amala; Dallas-area favorite. Delivery available.

name: Tailat Kitchen
about: Authentic Jollof & Whole Grilled Tilapia.

name: Lady T Kitchen
about: Major local hub for traditional swallows.

name: Bamboo Walk
about: Upscale Black-owned Caribbean spot; traditional Jamaican and Haitian flavors. Famous for East Flatbush vibes, drive-thru, and weekend brunch.

name: Cocobreeze
about: Oakland's only Trinidadian; roti wraps, curry goat pelau, pholourie. Chef Ann; Wed–Sat 11am–8pm.

name: BlackStar Kebab
about: Seattle's premier Ghanaian spot. Food truck with traditional family recipes—chicken, beef, lamb kebabs, jollof rice, sweet plantains; organic ingredients.

name: 48th Street Grille
about: West Philly institution. Casual Caribbean & American; BYOB (wine/champagne/beer); catering; Visit Philly Top Cajun & Caribbean.

name: Tsehay Ethiopian Restaurant & Bar
about: Woman-owned; Michelin-recognized. Fresh teff injera, tibs, shiro; strong vegan menu. Adams Morgan.

name: Feru Ethiopian Cuisine
about: Opened 2023; brunch & traditional dishes. Family recipes from owner's mother (30+ years).

name: Little Lagos
address: 3979 Buford Hwy NE Suite 112, Atlanta, GA 30345
about: ATL's #1 Nigerian; jollof, egusi, beef suya. Late hours, outdoor seating. Black-owned.

name: Baba Jollof
address: 8330 W Bellfort Ave Ste C, Houston, TX 77071
about: Authentic Nigerian; jollof, ayamase, egusi, okro. BYOB, vegan options.

name: Komchop
about: Some of the best jollof in Houston; banga, soups. Counter service, large portions.
vibe: Fast-casual Nigerian bowls and mains — easy daily driver pick for Houston.

name: Native Pot Restaurant
about: Jollof, dodo, seasoned meats and fish made fresh daily.

name: Vee's
about: 30+ years; oxtail, cow foot soup, ackee & saltfish. Generous portions, meals from $7. Crown Heights.
vibe: Casual counter-service Jamaican — patties, bowls, and platters locals use as a daily driver spot.

name: Irie Caribbean Kitchen
about: Best Jamaican in Bed-Stuy; oxtail, generous portions. Delivery & dine-in.

name: Tesfa Ethiopian Cuisine
about: Family-run; injera, kitfo, tibs. Edgewater. Vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free options.

name: Badou Senegalese Cuisine
about: Yassa chicken, mafe, jollof. Vegan options. Rogers Park.

name: Two Hommés
address: 902 N La Brea Ave, Inglewood, CA 90302
about: Afro-centric; California meets West African. Honey berbere chicken, jollof, root beer short ribs. Thu–Mon.

name: Blessed Tropical Jamaican Cuisine
about: Black-owned, veteran-owned. Oxtail, curry goat, jerk chicken. Outdoor seating, happy hour.

name: Selam Ethiopian Kitchen
about: Communal platters, organic ingredients. Full bar, late-night Fri–Sat. Uptown.

name: Abyssinia Restaurant and Cafe
address: 842 E Indian School Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85014
about: Classic Ethiopian plates, injera, and vegan options in central Phoenix.
our_story: Serving the Valley with traditional recipes and Ethiopian coffee ceremony.
cultural_roots: Habesha hospitality and shared platters.

name: Authentic Ethio African Kitchen & Bar
about: Ethiopian kitchen and bar near the heart of Phoenix with takeout and delivery.
our_story: Focused on spice-forward stews and fresh injera for the downtown corridor.
cultural_roots: East African comfort food for the diaspora community.

name: Ethiopian Famous Restaurant
about: Tempe-area Ethiopian spot known for vegetarian combos and hearty meat stews.
our_story: Carrying Habesha flavors for students and families near ASU.
